name: Cream of Sun-Dried Tomato Soup
recipeIngredient: 1 quart Milk, whole, low-fat or skim
1 small Onion, peeled and tied with 2 Cloves
4 tbsp Rice flour (or barley or oat flour)
Chopped Herbs for garnish (basil, chervil or parsley)
recipeInstructions:
This creamy, vibrant red soup has but 100 calories per serving if you use low-fat milk and no cream.
1. Place the milk, onion, peppercorns, salt and bouquet garni in a saucepan and bring slowly to a boil.
2. Form a smooth paste of the rice flour and cold milk. Stir briskly into the just-boiling milk until there are no lumps.
3. Simmer over very low heat for 20 minutes.
4. Meanwhile, boil the sun-dried tomatoes in the 2 cups water; do not drain. Strain the milk mixture, add the tomatoes and their liquid, and simmer another 5 minutes.
5. In a blender, liquefy the soup in batches.
6. Return to the stove and bring just to a boil.
7. Add the optional cream, if desired, and serve in hot bowls garnished with minced green herb.
